calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Filing Of Receipts Signed By The Distributees/Heirs
Off Calendar Aug-23-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam, via ZOOM, for Garey DeMartini, Executor; Thomas Cain, via ZOOM, for Fraser and Fraser, Assignee; John McGrath, via ZOOM, for Beth Hansen, Objector.

The court advises that it would feel more comfortable with funds being directly wired to the nine (9) heirs residing in Italy. Mr. Lydon-Lam advises that he will work with Mr. Cain to ensure that funds are wired and points to the petition wherein the heirs request funds be issued via checks. Mr. Cain reports that some of the heirs do not have bank accounts and offers additional testimony to address the court's concerns and advises that his firm has made distributions to heirs via the firms trust account. The court and Mr. Cain continue to discuss his firm's history of handling cases like this one. The court reminds counsel that its responsibility is to ensure that the heirs receive their distributions and invites Mr. Cain to file a declaration regarding his firm's responsibility for the administration of the distributions. The court confirms with Mr. McGrath that it has the most recent proposed order that reflects the correct distribution for Albina DeMartini.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-28-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Cain appeared by zoom for Fraser & Fraser (Petioner).
Bobby Lydon-Lam appeared by zoom for Garey DeMartini (Administrator).

The Court noted that there is no notice to the three children of Albina DeMartini. Mr. Cain stated that they have not been filed, that he just received them, that there is difficulty in getting things from Italy due to COVID and that he can file them immediately. The Court stated that there is no statutory authority under which the petition for approval of the settlement agreement should be approved by the Court and that it doesn't know the standard. Mr. Cain stated that the standard is that they have the identity of the heirs, that they have consents from the heirs to file for approval of the settlement, and that they are asking to distribute under Probate Codes 6402 and 240. The Court stated that it only knows of Probate Code 9837 and questions how this agreement is an advantage to the estate. Mr. Cain stated that he has done hundreds of these petitions and has never been asked, that he does know that it's in the best interest of the estate, that there is the issue of international and private law, that the heirs are in Italy, and that there is a claim in Italy with very complex issues of fact and law that will have to be decided in Italy. Mr. Cain also stated that there is an advantage to the estate in costs and uncertainty as to facts and law as to how it will be decided, and that it saves time. Mr. Lydon-Lam stated that he agrees with Mr. Cain and that he has no objections. The Court stated that they will not be able to close out the estate, that it will not be able to allow distribution to Albina's heirs and that it can only distribute to the estate of Albina. Mr. Cain stated that he filed a declaration which he believes addresses this issue, that the matter has already been determined in Italy, that there is no appointed Administrator, that it is governed by Italian law and asked if the Court would be willing to hold back that share. The Court stated that it disagrees. Mr. Cain confirmed that settlement amount as to Franca comes off the top and that the rest will be distributed in proportions previously contemplated. The Court noted that the issue of where and how Albina's interest gets there isn't implicating the either one of these petitions. The Petition to Determine Heirship, and the Petition for Court Review and Confirmation of Settlement Agreement are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-29-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam appeared by zoom for Garey DeMartini (Administrator).

Mr. Lydon-Lam stated that he has a couple of pending petitions (Petition to Determine Heirship and Petition for Confirmation of Settlement Agreement) on calendar for November 10, 2020 and that he is hoping to have today's status hearing continued to that date. The Court informed Mr. Lydon-Lam that this is not the way this works and that he needs to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Status on close of estate is continued to August 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lydon-Lam is to have his petition filed by August 19, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam for Garey DeMartini.

Mr. Lam stated that he has not learned anything more about the maternal heirs, that Counsel for the heir search company could not appear due to a conflict and that notice has been given to all of the heirs known to them. The Court noted that it has not seen any assignments and informed Counsel that he will need to file a petition to determine entitlement prior to filing a petition for final distribution. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $6,645,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for May 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
